long_reaction_chains.name: Long Reaction Chains
long_reaction_chains.description: Questions involving multiple sequential steps, reagents, or transformations.

structural_patterns: Product of one step becomes reactant of next

counting_enumeration.name: Counting/Enumeration
counting_enumeration.description: Questions requiring counting of molecules, ions, bonds, or specific numerical calculations.

deep_mechanistic_reasoning.name: Deep Mechanistic Reasoning
deep_mechanistic_reasoning.description: Questions requiring understanding of reaction mechanisms, intermediates, or transition states.

comparative_ranking.name: Comparative Ranking
comparative_ranking.description: Questions requiring comparison of properties (acidity, basicity, reactivity, etc.) and arranging in orders.

questions.question_text: 30 mg is the same mass as: a) 300 decigrams b) 0.3 grams c) 0.0003 kg d) 0.03 g

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Simple unit conversion using metric prefixes.

questions.question_text: Orbital angular momentum depends on: a) l b) n and m c) m and s d) n and l
questions.solution_summary: Orbital angular momentum depends on the azimuthal quantum number (l).

questions.question_text: Cathode rays are deflected by: a) thin metal foils only b) magnetic field only c) electric and magnetic fields d) electric field only
questions.solution_summary: Cathode rays are negatively charged particles (electrons), so they are deflected by both electric and magnetic fields.

questions.question_text: For a cyclic process, the change in internal energy of the system is: a) zero b) always -ve c) One d) always +ve
questions.solution_summary: Internal energy is a state function. For a cyclic process, initial and final states are same, so ΔU = 0.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Concept of state functions and cyclic processes.

questions.question_text: The number of alkynes possible with molecular formula C5H8 is: a) 5 b) 2 c) 4 d) 3
questions.solution_summary: C5H8 alkynes: pent-1-yne, pent-2-yne, 3-methylbut-1-yne. Total 3 isomers.

questions.question_text: The only series of lines appear in the visible region of the electromagnetic spectrum of hydrogen is: a) Balmer series b) Pfund series c) Paschen series d) Lyman series
questions.solution_summary: Balmer series (n1=2) lies in the visible region. Lyman is UV, Paschen and Pfund are IR.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of spectral series and their regions.

questions.question_text: Choose one of the following in the order of increasing radii: (options for I, I+, I-) a) I+ < I- < I b) I < I+ < I- c) I- < I+ < I d) I- > I > I+
questions.solution_summary: Cation is smaller than neutral atom; anion is larger. Order: I+ < I < I- (increasing). So I- > I > I+.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Comparison of cationic, neutral, and anionic radii.

questions.question_text: ΔH° of combustion of methane is -X kJ mol^-1. The value of ΔU° is: a) ΔU° < ΔH° b) ΔU° = 0 c) ΔU° > ΔH° d) ΔU° < ΔU°
questions.solution_summary: CH4(g) + 2O2(g) → CO2(g) + 2H2O(l). Δn_g = 1 - 3 = -2. ΔH = ΔU + Δn_g RT, so ΔU = ΔH - Δn_g RT = -X - (-2)RT = -X + 2RT. Since 2RT > 0, ΔU > ΔH? Wait: ΔH = ΔU + Δn_g RT. Δn_g = -2, so ΔH = ΔU - 2RT → ΔU = ΔH + 2RT = -X + 2RT. Since -X + 2RT > -X, ΔU > ΔH. So option (c) is correct. But solution says (d) ΔU° < ΔH°? Let me check... Actually ΔH = ΔU + Δn_g RT. For combustion of methane: CH4 + 2O2 → CO2 + 2H2O(l). Δn_g = 1 - 3 = -2. So ΔH = ΔU - 2RT. Therefore ΔU = ΔH + 2RT. So ΔU > ΔH.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of ΔH = ΔU + Δn_g RT.

questions.question_text: No work is done on the system, but q amount of heat is taken out from the system and given to the surrounding. The change in internal energy of a system is: a) ΔU = Wad b) ΔU = -q c) ΔU = q - W d) ΔU = +q
questions.solution_summary: First law: ΔU = q + w. w = 0 (no work), q is negative (heat taken out), so ΔU = -q.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of first law with sign conventions.

questions.question_text: The synthesis of 3-octyne is achieved by subsequent stepwise reactions of sodium amide with an alkyne, and a bromoalkane. The bromoalkane and the other alkyne respectively are: a) BrCH2CH2CH2CH2CH3 and CH3CH2C≡CH b) BrCH2CH2CH2CH2CH3 and CH3C≡CH c) BrCH2CH2CH2CH3 and CH3CH2C≡CH d) BrCH2CH2CH3 and CH3C≡CH
questions.solution_summary: 3-Octyne (CH3CH2CH2C≡CCH2CH2CH3) is prepared by reacting sodium butynide (CH3CH2C≡CNa) with 1-bromobutane (CH3CH2CH2CH2Br).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of alkyne synthesis via sodium alkylide and alkyl halide.

questions.question_text: In Daniell cell, the direction of current is: a) opposite to the direction of electron flow b) towards centre c) in the same direction of electron flow d) towards anode
questions.solution_summary: Current flows opposite to electron flow (conventional current from cathode to anode, electrons from anode to cathode).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of current direction in electrochemical cells.

questions.question_text: In allene (C3H4), the type(s) of hybridisation of the carbon atoms is (are): a) sp2 and sp3 b) Only sp2 c) sp2 and sp d) sp and sp3
questions.solution_summary: Allene (CH2=C=CH2): terminal carbons are sp2 hybridised, central carbon is sp hybridised.

questions.question_text: Assertion (A): It is impossible to determine the exact position and exact momentum of an electron simultaneously. Reason (R): The path of an electron in an atom is clearly defined.
questions.solution_summary: Assertion is true (Heisenberg uncertainty principle). Reason is false: electron path is not defined.

questions.question_text: Assertion (A): Electron deficient species that can accept lone pair of electrons known as an electrophile. Reason (R): NH4+ is an electrophile.
questions.solution_summary: Assertion is true: electrophiles accept electron pairs. Reason is false: NH4+ is not an electrophile (it's a cation but not typically considered an electrophile; it's the conjugate acid of NH3).

questions.question_text: Assertion (A): Molecular weight of a compound is 44 if its vapour density is 22. Reason (R): Vapour density × 2 = Molecular weight.
questions.solution_summary: Both assertion and reason are true. Molecular weight = 2 × Vapour density = 2 × 22 = 44.

questions.question_text: Assertion (A): Ozonolysis of alkynes are faster than ozonolysis of alkenes. Reason (R): Reaction proceed by cyclic transition state are faster on alkynes.
questions.solution_summary: Assertion is false: ozonolysis of alkenes is faster than alkynes. Reason is also false: alkynes have stronger bonds.

questions.question_text: How many seconds are there in 2 days?
questions.solution_summary: 2 days = 2 × 24 hours × 60 minutes × 60 seconds = 172800 seconds.

questions.question_text: A reaction between ammonia and boron trifluoride is given below. Identify the acid and base in this reaction. Which theory explains it? What is the hybridization of B and N in the reactants?
questions.correct_answer: BF3 is Lewis acid, NH3 is Lewis base. Lewis theory explains it. B in BF3 is sp2 hybridised, N in NH3 is sp3 hybridised.
questions.solution_summary: BF3 accepts electron pair (Lewis acid), NH3 donates (Lewis base).

questions.question_text: Why does the ionization enthalpy gradually decreases in a group?
questions.correct_answer: Atomic size increases down the group, shielding increases, effective nuclear charge decreases, making it easier to remove electron.
questions.solution_summary: Trend explained by atomic size and shielding.

questions.question_text: Arrange the three isomers of pentane in increasing order of their boiling points. OR Alkynes on reduction with sodium in liquid ammonia form trans alkenes. Will the butene thus formed on the reduction of the 2-butyne show the geometrical isomerism?
questions.correct_answer: 2,2-Dimethylpropane < 2-Methylbutane < Pentane. OR: Yes, but-2-ene shows geometrical isomerism (cis-trans).
questions.solution_summary: Branching decreases boiling point. 2-Butyne reduction gives trans-but-2-ene which shows geometrical isomerism.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Effect of branching on boiling point and isomerism.

questions.question_text: Write the electronic configuration of the following ions: i. H- ii. Na+ iii. O2- iv. F-
questions.solution_summary: H- has 2 electrons (1s2). Na+ has 10 electrons (1s2 2s2 2p6). O2- and F- also have 10 electrons (isoelectronic with Ne).

questions.question_text: Explain the structure of CO3^2- ion in terms of resonance.
questions.correct_answer: CO3^2- has three equivalent resonance structures with delocalised electrons. All C-O bonds are equivalent.
questions.solution_summary: Resonance hybrid of three canonical forms, bond order 1.33 for each C-O bond.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of resonance in carbonate ion.

questions.question_text: Give the name and an atomic number of the inert gas atom in which the total number of d-electrons is equal to the difference in numbers of total p and s electrons.
questions.correct_answer: Kr (Z=36): config = 1s2 2s2 2p6 3s2 3p6 3d10 4s2 4p6. Total d = 10, p = 18, s = 8, difference = 10.
questions.solution_summary: Krypton has 10 d-electrons, difference between p and s electrons is 10.

questions.question_text: Consider the reactions: a. 6CO2(g) + 6H2O(l) → C6H12O6(aq) + 6O2(g). b. O3(g) + H2O2(l) → H2O(l) + 2O2(g). Why it is more appropriate to write these reactions as: a. 6CO2 + 12H2O(l) → C6H12O6(aq) + 6H2O(l) + 6O2(g). b. O3(g) + H2O2(l) → H2O(l) + O2(g) + O2(g). Also suggest a technique to investigate the path of the above redox reactions.
questions.correct_answer: a. Photosynthesis uses 12 H2O molecules (6 are produced back). b. O2 comes from both O3 and H2O2. Technique: use isotopic labelling (H2^18O or O3^18).
questions.solution_summary: Stoichiometric representation and isotopic labelling technique.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of reaction stoichiometry and isotopic studies.

questions.question_text: What do you mean by entropy? (a) If enthalpy of fusion and enthalpy of vaporisation of sodium metals are 2.6 and 98.2 kJ mol^-1 respectively, what is the enthalpy of sublimation of sodium? (b) Why in some reactions heat is evolved while some reactions take place only on the absorption of heat?
questions.sub_topic: Entropy, Enthalpy of Sublimation, Heat Changes
questions.correct_answer: Entropy: measure of randomness. ΔsubH = ΔfusH + ΔvapH = 100.8 kJ mol^-1. Heat evolved if products have lower enthalpy; absorbed if products have higher enthalpy.
questions.solution_summary: Definitions and calculations.

questions.question_text: Emission transitions in the Paschen series end at orbit n=3 and start from orbit n and can be represented as v = 3.29×10^15(Hz)[1/3^2 - 1/n^2]. Calculate the value of n if the transition is observed at 1285 nm. Find the region of the spectrum.
questions.solution_summary: Using v = c/λ, solve for n. λ=1285 nm = 1.285×10^-6 m, v=2.3346×10^14 s^-1. Solve: n=5. Paschen series is in IR.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of Rydberg formula for Paschen series.

questions.question_text: Calculate the molecular mass of the following: i. H2O ii. CO2 iii. CH4
questions.solution_summary: Sum of atomic masses.

questions.question_text: Assuming the water vapor to be a perfect gas, calculate the internal energy change when 1 mol of water at 100°C and 1 bar pressure is converted to the ice at 0°C. Given the enthalpy of fusion of ice is 6.00 kJ mol^-1, heat capacity of water is 4.2 J/g°C.
questions.solution_summary: Cool water from 100°C to 0°C: ΔH1 = -18×4.2×100 = -7560 J = -7.56 kJ. Freeze at 0°C: ΔH2 = -6.00 kJ. Total ΔH = -13.56 kJ. Volume change negligible, so ΔU ≈ ΔH = -13.56 kJ.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Thermodynamic calculation involving cooling and phase change.

questions.question_text: Case: Purification of Organic Compounds. (a) Which method can be used to separate two compounds with different solubilities in a solvent? OR Distillation method is used to separate which type of substance? (b) Which technique is used to separate aniline from aniline water mixture? (c) Why chloroform and aniline are easily separated by the technique of distillation?
questions.correct_answer: (a) Fractional crystallisation. OR: volatile liquids from non-volatile impurities. (b) Steam distillation. (c) Sufficient difference in boiling points.
questions.solution_summary: Purification techniques based on properties.

questions.question_text: Case: Hybridisation. (a) Why all the orbitals in a set of hybridised orbitals have the same shape and energy? (b) Out of XeF2 and SF2 which molecule has the same shape as NO2+ ion? (c) Out of XeF4 and XeF2 which molecule doesn't have the same type of hybridisation as P(Phosphorus) has in PF5? OR Unsaturated compounds undergo additional reactions. Why?
questions.correct_answer: (a) Hybrid orbitals are equivalent. (b) XeF2 (linear, sp3d) has same shape as NO2+ (linear, sp). (c) XeF4 (sp3d2) doesn't have same hybridisation as PF5 (sp3d). OR: π bonds are weaker and can be broken for addition.
questions.solution_summary: Hybridisation and shape relationships.

questions.question_text: Attempt any five: (a) What effect does branching of an alkane chain has on its boiling point? (b) What are Arenes? (c) Write IUPAC name... (d) What are paraffins? (e) State Le Chatelier's principle. (f) What is decarboxylation? (g) Arrange HCl, HBr, HI, HF in order of decreasing reactivity towards alkenes.
questions.sub_topic: Boiling Points, Arenes, Le Chatelier, Decarboxylation, Reactivity
questions.correct_answer: (a) Branching decreases boiling point. (b) Aromatic hydrocarbons. (c) 5-(2-methylpropyl)decane. (d) Alkanes (little affinity). (e) If system at equilibrium is disturbed, it shifts to counteract the change. (f) Removal of CO2 from carboxylic acid salt. (g) HI > HBr > HCl > HF.
questions.solution_summary: Multiple organic and physical chemistry concepts.

questions.question_text: Answer: (i) i. 4-ethyl-3-propylhept-1-ene ii. 3-chloro-4-cyclopropyl-1,2-cyclobutanediol iii. Butan-2,3-diol (ii) 3-ethyl, 4-methylhept-5-en-2-one. (iii) 3-nitrocyclohex-1-ene. OR (i) Which of the given structures are functional group isomers? (ii) Why does electronegativity of carbon increase with increase in 's' character?
questions.sub_topic: IUPAC Nomenclature, Functional Group Isomers, Electronegativity
questions.correct_answer: OR: (i) I-V, I-VI, etc. are functional group isomers (alcohols and ethers). (ii) s electrons are closer to nucleus, so more 's' character means more electronegative.
questions.solution_summary: Nomenclature and conceptual questions.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Nomenclature and concept of functional group isomerism.

questions.question_text: Write the expression for the equilibrium constant for the following reactions: (a) 2NOCl(g) ⇌ 2NO(g) + Cl2(g) (b) 2Cu(NO3)2(s) ⇌ 2CuO(s) + 4NO2(g) + O2(g) (c) CH3COOC2H5(aq) + H2O(l) ⇌ CH3COOH(aq) + C2H5OH(aq) (d) Fe3+(aq) + 3OH-(aq) ⇌ Fe(OH)3(s) (e) I2(s) + 5F2(g) ⇌ 2IF5(l). OR (a) Describe the effect of: (i) addition of H2 (ii) addition of CH3OH (iii) removal of CO (iv) removal of CH3OH on the equilibrium of the reaction 2H2(g) + CO(g) ⇌ CH3OH(g). (b) What happens to equilibrium if a catalyst is added?
questions.sub_topic: Equilibrium Constant Expressions and Le Chatelier's Principle
questions.correct_answer: Kc expressions: (a) [NO]^2[Cl2]/[NOCl]^2 (b) [NO2]^4[O2] (c) [CH3COOH][C2H5OH]/[CH3COOC2H5] (d) 1/([Fe3+][OH-]^3) (e) 1/[F2]^5. OR: (i) shifts forward (ii) backward (iii) backward (iv) forward. Catalyst: no change in equilibrium position, only faster attainment.
questions.solution_summary: Equilibrium constant expressions and Le Chatelier's principle applications.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Writing Kc expressions and applying Le Chatelier's principle.
